So if they had ConnectionsActivity on top, they'd see it. With singleTop, when we start ConnectionsActivity from MainActivity, we have [MainActivity, ConnectionsActivity]. This intent is delivered to... the activity that has the matching intent filter. MainActivity (via alias) has that. So the system will bring the task to front. But which activity is shown? The one on top of the stack - ConnectionsActivity. So we should see ConnectionsActivity. Unless the document says that when the intent is delivered, the activity that matches might be brought to front (instead of the top activity). I will check Android docs... Actually I found: \"If the target activity has its launch mode set to \"singleTop\" in the manifest, and the target activity is already at the top of the stack, it will be reused and its onNewIntent() method will be called.\" So if the intent goes to MainActivity and MainActivity is NOT at the top (ConnectionsActivity is), then a new instance of MainActivity might be created and put on top! So we'd get [MainActivity, ConnectionsActivity, MainActivity] and the user would see MainActivity. That's the bug! So the fix is to set MainActivity's launchMode to \"singleTask\".             "lineNumber": 7999,
            "text": "2. Or the mutate in the useEffect (for restoring closed connections) has a condition that prevents restoring: \"if ((current.closedConnections?.length ?? 0) \u003e 0) return current\" - so we only inject closed from IndexedDB when current has no closed. But we don't restore activeConnections from anywhere - those come only from WebSocket. So when we re-enter, we have response.data = cached from SWR. If the cache was cleared (e.g. new session), we'd have initConnData (empty). So the fix could be: also persist activeConnections? No, that doesn't make sense - active connections are live. The issue might be that on Android when we navigate away the cache is lost, so when we come back we have empty. So we need to ensure the connection data is either kept in a global store (so it's not tied to the page mount), or we need to persist and restore the last snapshot so when we re-enter we show the last known state until the WebSocket reconnects and sends new data."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8000
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8001,
            "text": "Actually re-reading again: \"只有保持在链接界面才能看到item\" might mean \"only when staying on the connections interface can you see items\" - i.e. that's the current buggy behavior. So the user is saying: currently you can only see items when you stay on the connections page. They've \"closed\" (关闭) that - maybe they mean they've closed the connections page or they've disabled something. And the result is: when they re-enter the interface, there's nothing. So the fix would be: when re-entering the connections page, we should still see the items (e.g. by keeping the subscription or cache alive, or by restoring from storage)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8002
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8003,
            "text": "So the solution could be:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8004,
            "text": "1. Lift the connection data subscription to a higher level (e.g. app layout) so it stays active even when not on the connections page. Then when user opens the connections page, the data is already there."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8005,
            "text": "2. Or: persist the last connection snapshot (e.g. in localStorage or IndexedDB) and on connections page mount, show that until WebSocket connects and updates."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8006
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8007,
            "text": "Option 1 is cleaner but might have performance impact (always connected to connection WebSocket). Option 2: we could persist the full ConnectionMonitorData to IndexedDB when it updates, and on useConnectionData() mount we first try to load from IndexedDB (for both active and closed) so we show something immediately, then WebSocket updates."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8008
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8009,
            "text": "Let me check - currently we only persist closedConnections to IndexedDB. We don't persist activeConnections. So when the user leaves the page, the subscription unmounts and the in-memory data is gone. When they come back, we have initConnData (empty) until the first WebSocket message. On Android the WebSocket might take a moment to connect, so they see empty. So the fix: we could also persist the full snapshot (or at least activeConnections) to storage when we receive updates, and on mount restore from storage so we show the last snapshot immediately, then merge with live WebSocket data. That way re-entering shows the last known state."
          }

            "text": "    /** 在离开连接页时调用，仅持久化当前活跃快照（供返回时计算「因模式切换被关掉的连接」），已关闭列表不再写盘以省电。 */"

          },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 3336,
            "text": "   Unless: when the user switches mode, does the app **leave** ConnectionsActivity? If the user is on the proxy page and switches mode there, they're not on ConnectionsActivity. So ConnectionsActivity might be in the back stack with its design still in memory. But the ticker only runs while the select is running - and that's in the main() of ConnectionsActivity. So when the user navigates away from ConnectionsActivity to switch mode, does the activity get destroyed? If the activity is destroyed, the coroutine main() is cancelled. When the user comes back to ConnectionsActivity, a new instance is created, new ConnectionsDesign, lastSnapshot = null. Then refreshConnections is called, we get the current snapshot (already after mode switch, fewer connections). We have previous = null, so we don't add anything to closed. That's the bug!"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3337
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3338,
            "text": "   So the fix is: persist the last snapshot (or at least the list of connection IDs) when we update it, and when patchConnections is called with previous == null, load the last snapshot from storage and use it as \"previous\" for the purpose of computing newly closed. We need to save the snapshot to storage in patchConnections (e.g. when we have a valid snapshot), and load it when previous is null."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3339
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3340,
            "text": "   Let me implement:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3341,
            "text": "   - Add a \"last connections snapshot\" storage (similar to ClosedConnectionsStorage). We can store the ConnectionsSnapshot or just the list of connection IDs with minimal info. Actually we need the full connection data to add to closedEntries, so we need to store the full snapshot (or at least the connections that were in the previous snapshot). So we persist lastSnapshot (or its connections) whenever we update lastSnapshot. When we load, we need to load the previous snapshot. File: e.g. last_connections_snapshot.json or we could add to an existing storage. Let me create LastConnectionsSnapshotStorage or add to ClosedConnectionsStorage. Actually better a separate file: last_connections_snapshot.json storing ConnectionsSnapshot (or a simplified form with connection list)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3342
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3343,
            "text": "   Check the core model ConnectionsSnapshot and Connection for serialization."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 3343
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 3334
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\.specstory\\history\\2026-02-07_08-03-10Z-android-closed-connections-list-and-controls.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3579787611961365
    },

            "text": "  // 重新进入连接页时从 IndexedDB 恢复上次快照（活跃+已关闭），避免列表空白；若无快照则仅恢复已关闭列表"

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 5519,
            "text": "2. To do that we need to call a method on ConnectionsDesign from ConnectionsActivity's onPause or when the activity is about to be destroyed. But wait - when the user switches to another app or to the proxy screen to change mode, the ConnectionsActivity might go to onPause. So we could persist in onPause. When they come back (onResume), we have ActivityStart event which triggers refreshConnections. So the flow would be: user leaves connection screen -\u003e onPause -\u003e we persist lastSnapshot (we need to get it from design - so design needs to expose lastSnapshot or a method persistSnapshot()). When they return, main() is still running (activity not destroyed), so lastSnapshot is still in memory - we don't need to load. So we only need to load when the activity was destroyed and recreated. So we need to persist when the activity is destroyed (onDestroy) or when it's stopped. And we only need to load when lastSnapshot is null (first patch after creation). So the key is: persist only when leaving the activity (onPause/onStop/onDestroy), not on every tick."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5520
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5521,
            "text": "Let me implement:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5522,
            "text": "1. Add a method on ConnectionsDesign like &#96;suspend fun persistLastSnapshotIfNeeded()&#96; or we need to save the snapshot from the activity. Actually the design holds lastSnapshot which is private. So we need either:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5523,
            "text": "   - ConnectionsDesign.persistLastSnapshot() that saves lastSnapshot to storage (and we call it from Activity.onPause/onStop). So we need to expose a method that saves whatever is in lastSnapshot."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5524,
            "text": "   - Or we could pass the snapshot from the activity - when we're about to pause, we have the latest snapshot from the last refreshConnections. So in ConnectionsActivity we could in onPause get the design and... we don't have the snapshot in the activity. The design has it. So we need design.persistLastSnapshot() which writes design's lastSnapshot to storage. And we call it from the activity's onPause."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5525
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5526,
            "text": "2. Remove the \"save on every patch\" - remove the line &#96;launch { LastConnectionsSnapshotStorage.save(context, snapshot) }&#96; from patchConnections."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5527
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5528,
            "text": "3. In ConnectionsActivity we need to call design.persistLastSnapshot() when the activity is paused. But ConnectionsActivity extends BaseActivity - we need to see if we have onPause and how to get the design reference. Let me check - setContentDesign(design) so the activity holds the design. We need to override onPause and call design.persistLastSnapshot(). But persistLastSnapshot needs to be suspend or blocking - and we're saving to disk. The storage save is suspend. So we need to launch a coroutine in onPause to do the save. So design.persistLastSnapshot() could be a suspend function that we call from a coroutine in onPause."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 5528
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 5518
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\.specstory\\history\\2026-02-07_08-03-10Z-android-closed-connections-list-and-controls.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.35385239124298096
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 3134,
            "text": "   One more possibility: when mode is switched, does the app **navigate away** from Connections or **recreate** the Activity? If ConnectionsActivity is destroyed and recreated when switching mode, then when we come back we have a **new** ConnectionsDesign with lastSnapshot = null. So the first patchConnections(snapshot) has previous = null, and we don't add anything to closed. That would explain the bug!"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3135
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3136,
            "text": "   So the fix would be: when we have previous == null (first time we get a snapshot after opening the screen), we should not assume all current connections are \"new\". But we're not adding current to closed when previous is null - we're only adding to closed when previous is non-null and connection is in previous but not in current. So the issue is: when we have a new design (lastSnapshot = null), we receive the first snapshot which is already the \"after switch\" state (few connections). We don't add the closed ones to closedEntries because we never had them in lastSnapshot."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3137
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3138,
            "text": "   So the fix: we need to persist **lastSnapshot** (or at least the list of connection IDs that were active) when the activity is destroyed or when the app goes to background, so that when we come back and get a new snapshot, we can compute the diff. Alternatively, we could persist the \"active connections\" somewhere (e.g. when we receive each snapshot we write it to a file), and on the next patch we load that as \"previous\" if our in-memory previous is null. So:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3139,
            "text": "   - When patchConnections(snapshot) is called with previous == null, try to load the last known snapshot from storage. If we have it, use it as previous for the purpose of computing newly closed. Then update lastSnapshot to current snapshot."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3140,
            "text": "   - We need to persist the snapshot (or at least connection list) whenever we receive it, and clear it when the app is killed or when we don't need it."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3141
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3142,
            "text": "   Let me check if there's already something we can use. We have ClosedConnectionsStorage for closed entries. We could add a \"last active snapshot\" storage: when we patch, we save the current snapshot's connection IDs (or full snapshot) to disk. When we patch with previous == null, we load from disk and use that as the previous for computing closed. Then we save the new snapshot. This way, when the activity is recreated after a mode switch, we load the \"last snapshot\" from before (which had many connections), compare with the new snapshot (few connections), and add the diff to closed."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3143
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3144,
            "text": "   I'll implement that."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3145
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3146,
            "text": "   Now for the two buttons on Android:"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 3146
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 3132
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\.specstory\\history\\2026-02-07_08-03-10Z-android-closed-connections-list-and-controls.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3504537045955658

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 7824,
            "text": "The user wants: when selecting Rule mode, it should be equivalent to \"not modifying\" - so we shouldn't show the toast, and we might not even need to patch (or we patch with null/rule to clear the session override). Looking at the native code, when mode is \"rule\" or default, patchDirectGlobalMode doesn't apply any override. So when user selects Rule:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7825,
            "text": "- We should NOT show the toast (只在当前会话生效)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7826,
            "text": "- We could clear the session override for mode so it's truly \"no override\", or we still set mode = Rule in session override - the native side will then not apply direct/global overrides. So the behavior is correct if we set mode = Rule; the only fix is to not show the toast when mode is Rule."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7827
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7828,
            "text": "So fix 1: In ProxyActivity.kt, only call design.showModeSwitchTips() when it.mode is not null and not Rule (i.e. when it's Global or Direct)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7829
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7830,
            "text": "Now for the second issue: \"安卓已关闭只有保持在链接界面才能看到item，如果重新进入界面，就什么也没有了\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7831
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7832,
            "text": "So the user disabled some option that was \"only show items when staying on the connections page\" - meaning they want to see items even when they leave and come back. But when they re-enter the connections page, the list is empty."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7833
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7834,
            "text": "This could be:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7835,
            "text": "1. On Android the connection WebSocket might be tied to the connections page - when you leave the page the component unmounts and the subscription is cleaned up. When you come back, a new subscription starts with fresh state (initConnData), and the closed connections are restored from IndexedDB asynchronously. So active connections would be empty until the next WebSocket message arrives. So the issue might be that on re-entry we have no data yet."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7836,
            "text": "2. Or there's an option somewhere \"only fetch connection when on connections page\" - when disabled, maybe the app stops subscribing when leaving the page, and when re-entering the subscription key or state is wrong so we get empty."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7837
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7838,
            "text": "Let me check - in the frontend, useConnectionData() is used only in the connections page (connections.tsx). So when the user navigates away from the connections page, the ConnectionsPage unmounts, so useConnectionData() unmounts, and the SWR subscription cleanup runs (closes WebSocket, etc.). When they come back, useConnectionData() runs again with the same storageKey \"mihomo_connection_date\" - the date comes from useLocalStorage so it's the same. So subscriptKey is the same. So we get the same cache key. But the subscription might re-run from scratch - the subscribe function runs again, which creates a new WebSocket connection. The fallbackData is initConnData (empty). So until the first WebSocket message arrives, response.data is the fallbackData (empty). And there's keepPreviousData = true - so actually SWR subscription might keep the previous data when revalidating. Let me check useSWRSubscription behavior..."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7839
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7840,
            "text": "Actually re-reading the user message: \"安卓已关闭只有保持在链接界面才能看到item\" - I think they're saying they have disabled the option that restricts showing items to only when staying on the connection page. So now they expect to see items even when they leave and come back. But when they re-enter, they see nothing."
          }
        ],

            "text": "Let me check - in the frontend, useConnectionData() is used only in the connections page (connections.tsx). So when the user navigates away from the connections page, the ConnectionsPage unmounts, so useConnectionData() unmounts, and the SWR subscription cleanup runs (closes WebSocket, etc.). When they come back, useConnectionData() runs again with the same storageKey \"mihomo_connection_date\" - the date comes from useLocalStorage so it's the same. So subscriptKey is the same. So we get the same cache key. But the subscription might re-run from scratch - the subscribe function runs again, which creates a new WebSocket connection. The fallbackData is initConnData (empty). So until the first WebSocket message arrives, response.data is the fallbackData (empty). And there's keepPreviousData = true - so actually SWR subscription might keep the previous data when revalidating. Let me check useSWRSubscription behavior..."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7839
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7840,
            "text": "Actually re-reading the user message: \"安卓已关闭只有保持在链接界面才能看到item\" - I think they're saying they have disabled the option that restricts showing items to only when staying on the connection page. So now they expect to see items even when they leave and come back. But when they re-enter, they see nothing."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7841
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7842,
            "text": "So the flow is: "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7843,
            "text": "- Option A: \"Only show connection items when on connection page\" - when this is ON, maybe we only subscribe when on the page (current behavior). When OFF, maybe we subscribe globally so data is always there - but that might not be implemented, so when OFF and they re-enter, something is broken."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7844,
            "text": "- Option B: There's no such option in the code I've seen. So maybe the user means: on Android, the connection list is empty when they re-enter the page. So the issue is that when the connections page unmounts (user navigates away), the WebSocket closes and the in-memory state is lost. When they come back, the hook re-runs but the SWR cache might be re-used with the same key - let me check."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7845
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7846,
            "text": "useSWRSubscription with key &#96;subscriptKey&#96; = &#96;getClashConnection-${date}&#96;. The key is stable (date from localStorage). When the component unmounts, the subscription's cleanup runs (close WebSocket). When the component mounts again, useSWRSubscription runs again with the same key. So SWR might still have the previous data in cache for that key! So we might get the old data back. Unless on Android the cache is cleared or the key changes. Or the issue is that the subscription when it re-runs does \"if (wsFirstConnectionRef.current || !wsRef.current)\" - wsFirstConnectionRef is reset to true in cleanup, so we always do cleanupAll() and connectWs(). So we reconnect. And the initial state we pass to the subscriber might be from cache (keepPreviousData: true). So when we have keepPreviousData, the &#96;response&#96; might still have the previous data. So actually the list might not be empty unless something clears the cache."
          },

          },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 17802,
            "text": "- **修改**："
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17803,
            "text": "  - **布局** &#96;design/src/main/res/layout/adapter_connection_item.xml&#96;：第一行改为水平 LinearLayout，左侧为主机/链，右侧新增一个用于显示协议的 TextView，绑定变量 &#96;networkDisplayText&#96;。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17804,
            "text": "  - **Adapter** &#96;design/src/main/java/com/github/kr328/clash/design/adapter/ConnectionAdapter.kt&#96;：在 &#96;onBindViewHolder&#96; 里设置 &#96;holder.binding.networkDisplayText = conn.metadata?.network?.takeIf { it.isNotEmpty() }?.uppercase()&#96;（有值则显示如 TCP/UDP，无则隐藏）。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17805,
            "text": "- 效果：每条连接右侧会显示协议类型（如 TCP、UDP）。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17806
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17807,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17808
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17809,
            "text": "**3. 从后台返回时连接界面被主界面替代**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17810
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17811,
            "text": "- **原因**：MainActivity 使用 &#96;singleTop&#96;。从桌面/启动器再次点图标时，系统会把 MAIN/LAUNCHER 的 Intent 发给 MainActivity，若 MainActivity 不在栈顶（例如当前是 ConnectionsActivity），会再启动一个 MainActivity 压栈，用户看到的是主界面。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17812,
            "text": "- **修改**（&#96;app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml&#96;）：将 MainActivity 的 &#96;android:launchMode=\"singleTop\"&#96; 改为 &#96;android:launchMode=\"singleTask\"&#96;。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17813,
            "text": "- 效果：从后台返回时，会回到当前任务栈顶的界面；若之前在连接页，会继续停留在连接页，而不是被主界面盖住。"

          {
            "lineNumber": 17815,

          {
            "lineNumber": 17816
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17817,
            "text": "请重新编译并安装 APK 验证上述行为。若你希望“从桌面点图标也直接回到上次的界面（如连接页）”，当前 &#96;singleTask&#96; 的改法已经满足：点图标会把已有任务提到前台，栈顶是 Connections 就仍显示连接界面。"

            "text": "---"

            "lineNumber": 17820
          },
          {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 8007,
            "text": "Option 1 is cleaner but might have performance impact (always connected to connection WebSocket). Option 2: we could persist the full ConnectionMonitorData to IndexedDB when it updates, and on useConnectionData() mount we first try to load from IndexedDB (for both active and closed) so we show something immediately, then WebSocket updates."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8008
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8009,
            "text": "Let me check - currently we only persist closedConnections to IndexedDB. We don't persist activeConnections. So when the user leaves the page, the subscription unmounts and the in-memory data is gone. When they come back, we have initConnData (empty) until the first WebSocket message. On Android the WebSocket might take a moment to connect, so they see empty. So the fix: we could also persist the full snapshot (or at least activeConnections) to storage when we receive updates, and on mount restore from storage so we show the last snapshot immediately, then merge with live WebSocket data. That way re-entering shows the last known state."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8010
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8011,
            "text": "Let me implement both fixes:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8012
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8013,
            "text": "1. Android ProxyActivity: only show \"只在当前会话生效\" when mode is Global or Direct, not when mode is Rule."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8014,
            "text": "2. Connection list: Persist the full connection data (or active + closed) so when the user re-enters the connections page we can restore from storage and show the last snapshot until WebSocket reconnects. And/or lift the subscription to a global level so it doesn't unmount when leaving the page - but that might be a bigger change. The simpler fix is: in useConnectionData, on initial load restore from IndexedDB not just closedConnections but also the last known activeConnections if we have it stored. So we need to store the full snapshot in IndexedDB when we update, and on mount restore it. That way when they re-enter, we first restore from IndexedDB (so we have something to show), then the WebSocket connects and we get live updates."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8015
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8016,
            "text": "Actually re-reading the use-connection-data again - we have an effect that restores closedConnections from IndexedDB when subscriptionCacheKey is set. The condition is \"if ((current.closedConnections?.length ?? 0) \u003e 0) return current\" - so we only inject closed from storage when current has no closed. So when we mount fresh, current is initConnData (empty closedConnections), so we do inject closed from IndexedDB. So closed connections should appear. But activeConnections are never persisted - they're only in memory. So when we unmount and remount, activeConnections is empty. So the \"nothing\" the user sees might be when they're on the \"active\" tab - we have no active connections to show until the next WebSocket message. So the fix is to also persist activeConnections (the last snapshot) and restore it on mount, so when re-entering we show the last known active list until the WebSocket updates."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8017,
            "text": "\u003c/details\u003e\u003c/think\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8018
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8019,
            "text": "_**Agent (default)**_"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8020
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8021
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8022,
            "text": "Implementing two fixes:"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 8022
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 8005
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\.specstory\\history\\2026-02-07_04-16-04Z-安卓规则模式与界面显示问题.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3268285095691681
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 8594,
            "text": "\u003c/details\u003e\u003c/think\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8595
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8596,
            "text": "_**Agent (default)**_"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8597
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8598
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8599,
            "text": "修改已经按你的两个需求做完，总结如下："
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8600
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8601,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8602
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8603,
            "text": "### 1. 规则模式不再弹出「只在当前会话生效」"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8604
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8605,
            "text": "**文件：** &#96;app/src/main/java/com/github/kr328/clash/ProxyActivity.kt&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8606
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8607,
            "text": "- 只有切换到 **全局** 或 **直连** 时才调用 &#96;showModeSwitchTips()&#96;。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8608,
            "text": "- 选择 **规则模式** 时不再弹出「仅在本次会话中有效」，因为规则模式等于使用默认配置、不修改会话覆盖。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8609
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8610,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8611
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8612,
            "text": "### 2. 重新进入连接页时列表不再为空"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8613
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8614,
            "text": "**原因：** 连接页用 WebSocket 拉取数据，离开页面后订阅会销毁，再进来时先以空数据渲染，等 WebSocket 重连才有数据，所以会短暂看到“什么都没有”。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8615
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8616,
            "text": "**改动：**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8617
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8618,
            "text": "- **&#96;clash-verge-rev/src/utils/closed-connections-storage.ts&#96;**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8619,
            "text": "  - 增加 &#96;ConnectionSnapshot&#96; 类型和 &#96;getConnectionSnapshot()&#96; / &#96;setConnectionSnapshot()&#96;。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8620,
            "text": "  - 用 IndexedDB 存「完整快照」（活跃连接 + 已关闭连接 + 流量合计），键为 &#96;\"snapshot\"&#96;。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8621
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8622,
            "text": "- **&#96;clash-verge-rev/src/hooks/use-connection-data.ts&#96;**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8623,
            "text": "  - **进入连接页时：** 先从 IndexedDB 读上次的完整快照；若有则用来恢复列表（活跃 + 已关闭），避免一进来是空的；若无快照再按原逻辑只恢复「已关闭」列表。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8624,
            "text": "  - **有数据时：** 每次从 WebSocket 得到新数据就调用 &#96;setConnectionSnapshot(data)&#96;，把当前状态写入 IndexedDB。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8625,
            "text": "  - **清除已关闭连接时：** 在清空已关闭列表后，用新的数据（含空 closed）再写一次快照，保持和当前展示一致。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8626
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8627,
            "text": "这样在安卓上关闭「只有保持在链接界面才能看到 item」或离开再进连接页时，会先显示上次保存的快照，等 WebSocket 连上后再用新数据更新，不会出现一进来完全空白的情况。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8628
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8629,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8630
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8631,
            "text": "_**User**_"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8632
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8633,
            "text": "1. 选择不同模式的时候不再弹出「只在当前会话生效」"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8634
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8635,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8636
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8637,
            "text": "_**Agent (default)**_"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8638
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8639,
            "text": "\u003cthink\u003e\u003cdetails\u003e\u003csummary\u003eThought Process\u003c/summary\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8640
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8641,
            "text": "The user wants to remove the \"只在当前会话生效\" (Valid only for current session) toast entirely when switching modes - not just when selecting rule mode. So they want no toast at all when selecting any mode (rule, global, or direct)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8642
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8643,
            "text": "I previously made it so the toast only shows when switching to Global or Direct. Now they want: never show the toast when selecting any mode."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 8643
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 8593
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\.specstory\\history\\2026-02-07_04-16-04Z-安卓规则模式与界面显示问题.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3260937035083771
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 114,
            "text": "const ConnectionsPage = () =\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 115,
            "text": "  const { t } = useTranslation();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 116,
            "text": "  const [match, setMatch] = useState\u003c(input: string) =\u003e boolean\u003e("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117,
            "text": "    () =\u003e () =\u003e true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118,
            "text": "  );"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119,
            "text": "  const [curOrderOpt, setCurOrderOpt] = useState\u003cOrderKey\u003e(\"default\");"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120,
            "text": "  const [connectionsType, setConnectionsType] = useState\u003c\"active\" | \"closed\"\u003e("
          },
          {

          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 123,
            "text": "  const [mergeByDomain, setMergeByDomain] = useState(false);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 124
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 125,
            "text": "  const {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 126,
            "text": "    response: { data: connections },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 127,
            "text": "    clearClosedConnections,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 128,
            "text": "    sessionStartMs,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 129,
            "text": "  } = useConnectionData();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 130
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 131,
            "text": "  const [setting, setSetting] = useConnectionSetting();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 132
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 133,
            "text": "  const isTableLayout = setting.layout === \"table\";"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 134
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 135,
            "text": "  const [isColumnManagerOpen, setIsColumnManagerOpen] = useState(false);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 136
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 137,
            "text": "  /**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 138,
            "text": "   * 仅统计非直连（未走 DIRECT）、且在当前会话（sessionStartMs）之后建立的连接流量。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 139,
            "text": "   * 排除 sessionStartMs 之前的连接，使得重启软件或切换 TUN 模式后流量从 0 重新计算。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 140,
            "text": "   */"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 141,
            "text": "  const nonDirectTraffic = useMemo(() =\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 142,
            "text": "    const active = connections?.activeConnections ?? [];"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 143,
            "text": "    const closed = connections?.closedConnections ?? [];"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 144,
            "text": "    let download = 0;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 145,
            "text": "    let upload = 0;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 146,
            "text": "    for (const c of [...active, ...closed]) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 147,
            "text": "      if (c.chains?.includes?.(\"DIRECT\")) continue;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 148,
            "text": "      // Skip connections that were established before the current session started"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 149,
            "text": "      // (e.g. restored from IndexedDB cache from a previous run or pre-TUN-toggle session)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 150,
            "text": "      const connStartMs = new Date(c.start || 0).getTime();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 151,
            "text": "      if (connStartMs \u003c sessionStartMs) continue;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 152,
            "text": "      download += c.download ?? 0;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 153,
            "text": "      upload += c.upload ?? 0;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 154,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 155,
            "text": "    return { download, upload };"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 156,
            "text": "  }, [connections?.activeConnections, connections?.closedConnections, sessionStartMs]);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 157
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 158,
            "text": "  const [filterConn] = useMemo(() =\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 159,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": " */"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "object LastConnectionsSnapshotStorage {"
          },

            "text": "    private const val FILE_NAME = \"last_connections_snapshot.json\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {

            "text": "    private val json = Json { ignoreUnknownKeys = true }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "    fun file(context: Context): File = File(context.filesDir, FILE_NAME)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "    suspend fun load(context: Context): ConnectionsSnapshot? = withContext(Dispatchers.IO) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "        try {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "            val f = file(context)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "            if (!f.exists()) return@withContext null"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "            val text = f.readText()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "            if (text.isBlank()) return@withContext null"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "            json.decodeFromString(ConnectionsSnapshot.serializer(), text)"

          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "        } catch (_: Throwable) {"
          },
          {

          },
          {

            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "    suspend fun save(context: Context, snapshot: ConnectionsSnapshot) = withContext(Dispatchers.IO) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "        try {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "            val f = file(context)"
          },
          {

          {
            "lineNumber": 37,

            "lineNumber": 38,

这个问题是由 Android 的**启动模式（Launch Mode）**机制引起的，具体来说，是因为项目代码中将主界面配置为了 `singleTask` 模式。

以下是详细的原因分析：

### 1. `singleTask` 导致了“清栈”行为 (Clear Top)
在你的 `app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml` 文件中，`MainActivity`（主界面）被设置为了 `singleTask` 启动模式：

```xml:app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml
        <activity
            android:name=".MainActivity"
            android:configChanges="uiMode"
            android:exported="true"
            android:label="@string/launch_name"
            android:launchMode="singleTask"> <!-- 👈 问题出在这里 -->
```

在 Android 的设计中，`singleTask` 的完整语义是：“**如果该 Activity 已经存在于任务栈中，系统会将其上面的所有其他 Activity 全部销毁（出栈），使该 Activity 重新回到栈顶。**”

### 2. 你的操作场景还原
1. **进入连接界面**：此时你的界面栈是 `[主界面(MainActivity) -> 连接界面(ConnectionsActivity)]`。
2. **切到后台**：应用整体进入后台，栈结构保留。
3. **点击桌面图标返回**：当你点击桌面上的 App 图标时，Android 系统会发送一个启动 `MainActivity` 的指令（Launcher Intent）。
4. **闪回主界面**：系统发现 `MainActivity` 已经是 `singleTask`，于是立刻将压在它上面的 `ConnectionsActivity`（连接界面）**强制销毁**。这就导致了你看到的“闪一下”然后回到了主界面，而不是保留在连接界面。

*(注：如果你是通过手机的“最近任务/多任务卡片”切回应用，通常不会触发 Launcher Intent，此时系统只是将整个应用带回前台，一般就能保留在连接界面。但如果是被系统杀后台了或者是点击图标进入，必定会触发清栈。)*

### 3. 历史遗留问题
根据我查看项目里的历史记录（`.specstory\history\2026-01-31_05-01-20Z-安卓端连接与设置问题.md`），在 2026 年 1 月份的时候，AI 在尝试修复另一个多重界面的 Bug 时，**错误地理解了 Android 的官方文档**。

当时 AI 的思路是：*“把它设为 singleTask 就可以避免创建新的主界面，并且保留顶部的连接界面。”* —— 这是一个典型的理解误区，`singleTask` 恰恰会销毁顶部的界面。

### 💡 如何修复
由于我现在处于“问答模式（Ask Mode）”，我不能直接修改你的代码。如果你想解决这个问题，你可以切换回 Agent 模式让我帮你改，或者自己手动在 `app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml` 中进行如下修改：

将 `MainActivity` 的 `launchMode` 从 `singleTask` 改为 `singleTop`：

```xml
        <activity
            android:name=".MainActivity"
            android:configChanges="uiMode"
            android:exported="true"
            android:label="@string/launch_name"
            android:launchMode="singleTop"> <!-- 改回 singleTop 即可 -->
```

对于桌面图标启动而言，带有默认 `FLAG_ACTIVITY_RESET_TASK_IF_NEEDED` 标志的 Launcher Intent 配合 `singleTop` 就能完美实现“只把应用拉回前台，且保持在用户最后停留的界面（如连接界面）”，不会再把连接界面强制杀掉了。

Or the mutate in the useEffect (for restoring closed connections) has a condition that prevents restoring: \"if ((current.closedConnections?.length ?? 0) \u003e 0) return current\" - so we only inject closed from IndexedDB when current has no closed. But we don't restore activeConnections from anywhere - those come only from WebSocket. So when we re-enter, we have response.data = cached from SWR. If the cache was cleared (e.g. new session), we'd have initConnData (empty). So the fix could be: also persist activeConnections? No, that doesn't make sense - active connections are live. The issue might be that on Android when we navigate away the cache is lost, so when we come back we have empty. So we need to ensure the connection data is either kept in a global store (so it's not tied to the page mount), or we need to persist and restore the last snapshot so when we re-enter we show the last known state until the WebSocket reconnects and sends new data."

So the fix would be: when re-entering the connections page, we should still see the items (e.g. by keeping the subscription or cache alive, or by restoring from storage)."

          },

So when the user leaves the page, the subscription unmounts and the in-memory data is gone. When they come back, we have initConnData (empty) until the first WebSocket message. On Android the WebSocket might take a moment to connect, so they see empty. So the fix: we could also persist the full snapshot (or at least activeConnections) to storage when we receive updates, and on mount restore from storage so we show the last snapshot immediately, then merge with live WebSocket data. That way re-entering shows the last known state."

      "codeBlock": {

          {
            "lineNumber": 16964,
            "text": "\u003csummary\u003eTool use: **read_file_v2**\u003c/summary\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16965
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16966,
            "text": "\u003c/details\u003e\u003c/tool-use\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16967
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16968,
            "text": "_**Agent (default)**_"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16969
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16970,
            "text": "\u003cthink\u003e\u003cdetails\u003e\u003csummary\u003eThought Process\u003c/summary\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16971
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16972,
            "text": "I see MainActivity already has android:launchMode=\"singleTop\". So when user taps the app icon, the intent goes to MainActivity. With singleTop, if MainActivity is already in the task, it won't create a new one - but the intent is still delivered to MainActivity (onNewIntent). So the task is brought to front. If ConnectionsActivity was on top of the stack, we should see ConnectionsActivity. Unless... the launcher alias is MainActivityAlias which targets MainActivity. So when user taps icon, the intent might be to MainActivityAlias -\u003e MainActivity. The task would have MainActivity as the root. So when we bring task to front, we should see whatever activity is on top - ConnectionsActivity. So theoretically it should work. Unless the issue is that when the app is in background for a while, the process is killed. When user taps the icon again, a new task is created with MainActivity. So we get MainActivity. To preserve the \"last screen\", we could use android:launchMode=\"singleTask\" for MainActivity so that there's only one task, and when the task is recreated we'd still start with MainActivity. That doesn't help. The fix would be to save the \"current sub-activity\" in SharedPreferences or similar, and when MainActivity is started (onCreate), if we were on ConnectionsActivity before process death, we could start ConnectionsActivity. That's more complex. A simpler interpretation: maybe the issue is that when the activity is not destroyed but the app goes to background, when they return via recent apps they see MainActivity? That could happen if the task order is wrong. I will try singleTask for MainActivity - with singleTask, when user opens the app from launcher, the existing task is brought to front. So if they had ConnectionsActivity on top, they'd see it. With singleTop, when we start ConnectionsActivity from MainActivity, we have [MainActivity, ConnectionsActivity]. When user goes to background and taps icon, the intent is MAIN/LAUNCHER. The launcher might start MainActivityAlias. The target is MainActivity. So we have the same task. With singleTop, a new MainActivity is not created. So we get onNewIntent on MainActivity... but wait, the activity on top is ConnectionsActivity! So when the intent is delivered, which activity receives it? The launcher intent typically goes to the root activity of the task. So MainActivity might be brought to front? I need to check. Actually in Android, when you have task [MainActivity, ConnectionsActivity] and the user taps the app icon, the intent has action MAIN and category LAUNCHER. This intent is delivered to... the activity that has the matching intent filter. MainActivity (via alias) has that. So the system will bring the task to front. But which activity is shown? The one on top of the stack - ConnectionsActivity. So we should see ConnectionsActivity. Unless the document says that when the intent is delivered, the activity that matches might be brought to front (instead of the top activity). I will check Android docs... Actually I found: \"If the target activity has its launch mode set to \"singleTop\" in the manifest, and the target activity is already at the top of the stack, it will be reused and its onNewIntent() method will be called.\" So if the intent goes to MainActivity and MainActivity is NOT at the top (ConnectionsActivity is), then a new instance of MainActivity might be created and put on top! So we'd get [MainActivity, ConnectionsActivity, MainActivity] and the user would see MainActivity. That's the bug! So the fix is to set MainActivity's launchMode to \"singleTask\". With singleTask, when the intent is received, the task is brought to front and no new activity is created. So we'd see ConnectionsActivity. I will implement that."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16973
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16974,
            "text": "Actually wait - with singleTask, when we start ConnectionsActivity from MainActivity, we're starting a different activity. So the stack would be [MainActivity, ConnectionsActivity]. When user taps icon, intent goes to MainActivity. With singleTask, the system finds the task that has MainActivity. It brings that task to front. It does not create a new MainActivity because one already exists in the task. So the task [MainActivity, ConnectionsActivity] is brought to front, and the top activity ConnectionsActivity is visible. Perfect!"
          }
        ],

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 174,

          },

            "lineNumber": 175,
            "text": "    useMihomoWsSubscription\u003cConnectionMonitorData\u003e({"

          {

            "text": "  // 重新进入连接页时从 IndexedDB 恢复上次快照（活跃+已关闭），避免列表空白；若无快照则仅恢复已关闭列表"

            "lineNumber": 189,
            "text": "    private fun mergeConnectionsByHost(connections: List\u003cConnection\u003e): List\u003cConnection\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 190,
            "text": "        val byHost = connections.groupBy { it.metadata?.host?.takeIf { h -\u003e h.isNotEmpty() } ?: it.id }"

            "lineNumber": 191,
            "text": "        return byHost.values.map { group -\u003e"
          },

          },

            "text": "            val upload = group.sumOf { it.upload }"

          {

            "text": "            val download = group.sumOf { it.download }"

          {
            "lineNumber": 234,
            "text": "    /** 在离开连接页时调用，仅持久化当前活跃快照（供返回时计算「因模式切换被关掉的连接」），已关闭列表不再写盘以省电。 */"

          },

          },

            "lineNumber": 238
          },

          },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 3134,
            "text": "   One more possibility: when mode is switched, does the app **navigate away** from Connections or **recreate** the Activity? If ConnectionsActivity is destroyed and recreated when switching mode, then when we come back we have a **new** ConnectionsDesign with lastSnapshot = null. So the first patchConnections(snapshot) has previous = null, and we don't add anything to closed. That would explain the bug!"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3135
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3136,
            "text": "   So the fix would be: when we have previous == null (first time we get a snapshot after opening the screen), we should not assume all current connections are \"new\". But we're not adding current to closed when previous is null - we're only adding to closed when previous is non-null and connection is in previous but not in current. So the issue is: when we have a new design (lastSnapshot = null), we receive the first snapshot which is already the \"after switch\" state (few connections). We don't add the closed ones to closedEntries because we never had them in lastSnapshot."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3137
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3138,
            "text": "   So the fix: we need to persist **lastSnapshot** (or at least the list of connection IDs that were active) when the activity is destroyed or when the app goes to background, so that when we come back and get a new snapshot, we can compute the diff. Alternatively, we could persist the \"active connections\" somewhere (e.g. when we receive each snapshot we write it to a file), and on the next patch we load that as \"previous\" if our in-memory previous is null. So:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3139,
            "text": "   - When patchConnections(snapshot) is called with previous == null, try to load the last known snapshot from storage. If we have it, use it as previous for the purpose of computing newly closed. Then update lastSnapshot to current snapshot."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3140,
            "text": "   - We need to persist the snapshot (or at least connection list) whenever we receive it, and clear it when the app is killed or when we don't need it."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3141
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3142,
            "text": "   Let me check if there's already something we can use. We have ClosedConnectionsStorage for closed entries. We could add a \"last active snapshot\" storage: when we patch, we save the current snapshot's connection IDs (or full snapshot) to disk. When we patch with previous == null, we load from disk and use that as the previous for computing closed. Then we save the new snapshot. This way, when the activity is recreated after a mode switch, we load the \"last snapshot\" from before (which had many connections), compare with the new snapshot (few connections), and add the diff to closed."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3143
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3144,
            "text": "   I'll implement that."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3145
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3146,
            "text": "   Now for the two buttons on Android:"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 3146
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 3132
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\.specstory\\history\\2026-02-07_08-03-10Z-android-closed-connections-list-and-controls.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3665592670440674
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 7838,
            "text": "Let me check - in the frontend, useConnectionData() is used only in the connections page (connections.tsx). So when the user navigates away from the connections page, the ConnectionsPage unmounts, so useConnectionData() unmounts, and the SWR subscription cleanup runs (closes WebSocket, etc.). When they come back, useConnectionData() runs again with the same storageKey \"mihomo_connection_date\" - the date comes from useLocalStorage so it's the same. So subscriptKey is the same. So we get the same cache key. But the subscription might re-run from scratch - the subscribe function runs again, which creates a new WebSocket connection. The fallbackData is initConnData (empty). So until the first WebSocket message arrives, response.data is the fallbackData (empty). And there's keepPreviousData = true - so actually SWR subscription might keep the previous data when revalidating. Let me check useSWRSubscription behavior..."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7839
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7840,
            "text": "Actually re-reading the user message: \"安卓已关闭只有保持在链接界面才能看到item\" - I think they're saying they have disabled the option that restricts showing items to only when staying on the connection page. So now they expect to see items even when they leave and come back. But when they re-enter, they see nothing."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7841
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7842,
            "text": "So the flow is: "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7843,
            "text": "- Option A: \"Only show connection items when on connection page\" - when this is ON, maybe we only subscribe when on the page (current behavior). When OFF, maybe we subscribe globally so data is always there - but that might not be implemented, so when OFF and they re-enter, something is broken."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7844,
            "text": "- Option B: There's no such option in the code I've seen. So maybe the user means: on Android, the connection list is empty when they re-enter the page. So the issue is that when the connections page unmounts (user navigates away), the WebSocket closes and the in-memory state is lost. When they come back, the hook re-runs but the SWR cache might be re-used with the same key - let me check."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7845
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7846,
            "text": "useSWRSubscription with key &#96;subscriptKey&#96; = &#96;getClashConnection-${date}&#96;. The key is stable (date from localStorage). When the component unmounts, the subscription's cleanup runs (close WebSocket). When the component mounts again, useSWRSubscription runs again with the same key. So SWR might still have the previous data in cache for that key! So we might get the old data back. Unless on Android the cache is cleared or the key changes. Or the issue is that the subscription when it re-runs does \"if (wsFirstConnectionRef.current || !wsRef.current)\" - wsFirstConnectionRef is reset to true in cleanup, so we always do cleanupAll() and connectWs(). So we reconnect. And the initial state we pass to the subscriber might be from cache (keepPreviousData: true). So when we have keepPreviousData, the &#96;response&#96; might still have the previous data. So actually the list might not be empty unless something clears the cache."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7847
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7848,
            "text": "Let me search for any \"only when on connections page\" or \"connection\" visibility setting."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 7848
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 7836
          }
        },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 8594,
            "text": "\u003c/details\u003e\u003c/think\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8595
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8596,
            "text": "_**Agent (default)**_"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8597
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8598
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8599,
            "text": "修改已经按你的两个需求做完，总结如下："
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8600
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8601,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8602
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8603,
            "text": "### 1. 规则模式不再弹出「只在当前会话生效」"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8604
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8605,
            "text": "**文件：** &#96;app/src/main/java/com/github/kr328/clash/ProxyActivity.kt&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8606
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8607,
            "text": "- 只有切换到 **全局** 或 **直连** 时才调用 &#96;showModeSwitchTips()&#96;。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8608,
            "text": "- 选择 **规则模式** 时不再弹出「仅在本次会话中有效」，因为规则模式等于使用默认配置、不修改会话覆盖。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8609
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8610,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8611
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8612,
            "text": "### 2. 重新进入连接页时列表不再为空"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8613
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8614,
            "text": "**原因：** 连接页用 WebSocket 拉取数据，离开页面后订阅会销毁，再进来时先以空数据渲染，等 WebSocket 重连才有数据，所以会短暂看到“什么都没有”。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8615
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8616,
            "text": "**改动：**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8617
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8618,
            "text": "- **&#96;clash-verge-rev/src/utils/closed-connections-storage.ts&#96;**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8619,
            "text": "  - 增加 &#96;ConnectionSnapshot&#96; 类型和 &#96;getConnectionSnapshot()&#96; / &#96;setConnectionSnapshot()&#96;。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8620,
            "text": "  - 用 IndexedDB 存「完整快照」（活跃连接 + 已关闭连接 + 流量合计），键为 &#96;\"snapshot\"&#96;。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8621
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8622,
            "text": "- **&#96;clash-verge-rev/src/hooks/use-connection-data.ts&#96;**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8623,
            "text": "  - **进入连接页时：** 先从 IndexedDB 读上次的完整快照；若有则用来恢复列表（活跃 + 已关闭），避免一进来是空的；若无快照再按原逻辑只恢复「已关闭」列表。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8624,
            "text": "  - **有数据时：** 每次从 WebSocket 得到新数据就调用 &#96;setConnectionSnapshot(data)&#96;，把当前状态写入 IndexedDB。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8625,
            "text": "  - **清除已关闭连接时：** 在清空已关闭列表后，用新的数据（含空 closed）再写一次快照，保持和当前展示一致。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8626
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8627,
            "text": "这样在安卓上关闭「只有保持在链接界面才能看到 item」或离开再进连接页时，会先显示上次保存的快照，等 WebSocket 连上后再用新数据更新，不会出现一进来完全空白的情况。"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8628
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8629,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8630
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8631,
            "text": "_**User**_"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8632
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8633,
            "text": "1. 选择不同模式的时候不再弹出「只在当前会话生效」"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8634
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8635,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8636
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8637,
            "text": "_**Agent (default)**_"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8638
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8639,
            "text": "\u003cthink\u003e\u003cdetails\u003e\u003csummary\u003eThought Process\u003c/summary\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8640
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8641,
            "text": "The user wants to remove the \"只在当前会话生效\" (Valid only for current session) toast entirely when switching modes - not just when selecting rule mode. So they want no toast at all when selecting any mode (rule, global, or direct)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8642
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8643,
            "text": "I previously made it so the toast only shows when switching to Global or Direct. Now they want: never show the toast when selecting any mode."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 8643
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 8593
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\.specstory\\history\\2026-02-07_04-16-04Z-安卓规则模式与界面显示问题.md",

      },
      "score": 0.35564565658569336

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 8693,
            "text": "The user is saying that on Android, every time they open the connections page, the \"closed\" (已关闭) connections list is always empty - it gets cleared. So the closed connections are not being persisted/restored correctly on Android."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8694
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8695,
            "text": "Let me look at the flow again:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8696
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8697,
            "text": "1. We have &#96;setClosedConnectionsInStorage(closedConnections)&#96; called in &#96;mergeConnectionSnapshot&#96; in use-connection-data.ts - so whenever we get WebSocket updates and merge, we persist closed connections."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8698
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8699,
            "text": "2. We have &#96;setConnectionSnapshot(data)&#96; called in an effect when response.data has content - we persist the full snapshot (including closedConnections)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8700
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8701,
            "text": "3. On mount we have an effect that:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8702,
            "text": "   - First tries &#96;getConnectionSnapshot()&#96; - if it has data, we mutate with that snapshot (which includes closedConnections)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8703,
            "text": "   - Otherwise we call &#96;getClosedConnectionsFromStorage()&#96; and inject closed into the cache."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8704
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8705,
            "text": "Possible issues on Android:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8706,
            "text": "1. **IndexedDB might not be available or might behave differently** in the WebView on Android - e.g. different origin, or storage cleared when app is backgrounded."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8707,
            "text": "2. **Order of operations**: When the connections page mounts, the subscription might run and immediately set &#96;response.data&#96; to &#96;initConnData&#96; (empty) or to something from SWR cache. If the restore effect runs after the first WebSocket message, the WebSocket message might overwrite with fresh data from the core - and the core might not send \"closed\" connections in the API, so we'd get empty closedConnections from the WebSocket, and then we'd persist that empty list, overwriting our stored closed list."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8708,
            "text": "3. **mergeConnectionSnapshot** is called with payload from WebSocket. The payload is &#96;IConnections&#96; which typically has &#96;connections&#96; (active) and maybe &#96;uploadTotal&#96;/&#96;downloadTotal&#96;. Does it include closed connections? Usually the connections API only returns *active* connections - closed ones are tracked in the app. So when we get a WebSocket message we do:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8709,
            "text": "   - &#96;mergeConnectionSnapshot(parsed, old)&#96; - we compute newlyClosed from previousActive that disappeared, and we merge into closedConnections. So the \"closed\" list is maintained in memory by the merge. But at the end we call &#96;setClosedConnectionsInStorage(closedConnections)&#96; with the merged closed list. So we do persist."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 8709
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 8691
          }
        },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 7993,
            "text": "The user said \"安卓已关闭只有保持在链接界面才能看到item\" - I think they mean: On Android they have turned OFF some setting like \"only show items when staying on connections page\" (i.e. they want to see items even when they leave and come back). So the setting is \"closed\" (disabled). And the bug is: when they re-enter the connections interface, there's nothing (empty list)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7994
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7995,
            "text": "So the problem might be specific to Android/TAURI: when the connections page is not mounted, maybe the WebSocket isn't running (because the hook only runs when the page is mounted). So when they come back, we mount again, we create a new subscription. The SWR cache key is &#96;$sub$$getClashConnection-${date}&#96;. The &#96;date&#96; is from localStorage. So when we remount, we have the same key. With keepPreviousData: true, the previous data might still be in SWR's cache. So we might see the old data. Unless on Android the WebView or the app clears the cache when the page is not visible, or the subscription re-runs and immediately replaces with fallbackData before the cache is read."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7996
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7997,
            "text": "Looking at useSWRSubscription again - when the component unmounts, the cleanup runs. When it mounts again, the subscription runs again. The subscriptKey is the same. So SWR returns the cached data for that key. So response.data might be the previous cached data. So we should see the list. Unless:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7998,
            "text": "1. On Android the app doesn't keep the same SWR cache when navigating (e.g. the entire web view is recreated)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7999,
            "text": "2. Or the mutate in the useEffect (for restoring closed connections) has a condition that prevents restoring: \"if ((current.closedConnections?.length ?? 0) \u003e 0) return current\" - so we only inject closed from IndexedDB when current has no closed. But we don't restore activeConnections from anywhere - those come only from WebSocket. So when we re-enter, we have response.data = cached from SWR. If the cache was cleared (e.g. new session), we'd have initConnData (empty). So the fix could be: also persist activeConnections? No, that doesn't make sense - active connections are live. The issue might be that on Android when we navigate away the cache is lost, so when we come back we have empty. So we need to ensure the connection data is either kept in a global store (so it's not tied to the page mount), or we need to persist and restore the last snapshot so when we re-enter we show the last known state until the WebSocket reconnects and sends new data."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8000
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8001,
            "text": "Actually re-reading again: \"只有保持在链接界面才能看到item\" might mean \"only when staying on the connections interface can you see items\" - i.e. that's the current buggy behavior. So the user is saying: currently you can only see items when you stay on the connections page. They've \"closed\" (关闭) that - maybe they mean they've closed the connections page or they've disabled something. And the result is: when they re-enter the interface, there's nothing. So the fix would be: when re-entering the connections page, we should still see the items (e.g. by keeping the subscription or cache alive, or by restoring from storage)."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 8001

            "line": 7992

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 7824,
            "text": "The user wants: when selecting Rule mode, it should be equivalent to \"not modifying\" - so we shouldn't show the toast, and we might not even need to patch (or we patch with null/rule to clear the session override). Looking at the native code, when mode is \"rule\" or default, patchDirectGlobalMode doesn't apply any override. So when user selects Rule:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7825,
            "text": "- We should NOT show the toast (只在当前会话生效)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7826,
            "text": "- We could clear the session override for mode so it's truly \"no override\", or we still set mode = Rule in session override - the native side will then not apply direct/global overrides. So the behavior is correct if we set mode = Rule; the only fix is to not show the toast when mode is Rule."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7827
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7828,
            "text": "So fix 1: In ProxyActivity.kt, only call design.showModeSwitchTips() when it.mode is not null and not Rule (i.e. when it's Global or Direct)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7829
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7830,
            "text": "Now for the second issue: \"安卓已关闭只有保持在链接界面才能看到item，如果重新进入界面，就什么也没有了\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7831
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7832,
            "text": "So the user disabled some option that was \"only show items when staying on the connections page\" - meaning they want to see items even when they leave and come back. But when they re-enter the connections page, the list is empty."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7833
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7834,
            "text": "This could be:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7835,
            "text": "1. On Android the connection WebSocket might be tied to the connections page - when you leave the page the component unmounts and the subscription is cleaned up. When you come back, a new subscription starts with fresh state (initConnData), and the closed connections are restored from IndexedDB asynchronously. So active connections would be empty until the next WebSocket message arrives. So the issue might be that on re-entry we have no data yet."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7836,
            "text": "2. Or there's an option somewhere \"only fetch connection when on connections page\" - when disabled, maybe the app stops subscribing when leaving the page, and when re-entering the subscription key or state is wrong so we get empty."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7837
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7838,
            "text": "Let me check - in the frontend, useConnectionData() is used only in the connections page (connections.tsx). So when the user navigates away from the connections page, the ConnectionsPage unmounts, so useConnectionData() unmounts, and the SWR subscription cleanup runs (closes WebSocket, etc.). When they come back, useConnectionData() runs again with the same storageKey \"mihomo_connection_date\" - the date comes from useLocalStorage so it's the same. So subscriptKey is the same. So we get the same cache key. But the subscription might re-run from scratch - the subscribe function runs again, which creates a new WebSocket connection. The fallbackData is initConnData (empty). So until the first WebSocket message arrives, response.data is the fallbackData (empty). And there's keepPreviousData = true - so actually SWR subscription might keep the previous data when revalidating. Let me check useSWRSubscription behavior..."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7839
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7840,
            "text": "Actually re-reading the user message: \"安卓已关闭只有保持在链接界面才能看到item\" - I think they're saying they have disabled the option that restricts showing items to only when staying on the connection page. So now they expect to see items even when they leave and come back. But when they re-enter, they see nothing."
          }
        ],
