    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 8007,
            "text": "Option 1 is cleaner but might have performance impact (always connected to connection WebSocket). Option 2: we could persist the full ConnectionMonitorData to IndexedDB when it updates, and on useConnectionData() mount we first try to load from IndexedDB (for both active and closed) so we show something immediately, then WebSocket updates."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8008
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8009,
            "text": "Let me check - currently we only persist closedConnections to IndexedDB. We don't persist activeConnections. So when the user leaves the page, the subscription unmounts and the in-memory data is gone. When they come back, we have initConnData (empty) until the first WebSocket message. On Android the WebSocket might take a moment to connect, so they see empty. So the fix: we could also persist the full snapshot (or at least activeConnections) to storage when we receive updates, and on mount restore from storage so we show the last snapshot immediately, then merge with live WebSocket data. That way re-entering shows the last known state."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8010
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8011,
            "text": "Let me implement both fixes:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8012
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8013,
            "text": "1. Android ProxyActivity: only show \"只在当前会话生效\" when mode is Global or Direct, not when mode is Rule."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8014,
            "text": "2. Connection list: Persist the full connection data (or active + closed) so when the user re-enters the connections page we can restore from storage and show the last snapshot until WebSocket reconnects. And/or lift the subscription to a global level so it doesn't unmount when leaving the page - but that might be a bigger change. The simpler fix is: in useConnectionData, on initial load restore from IndexedDB not just closedConnections but also the last known activeConnections if we have it stored. So we need to store the full snapshot in IndexedDB when we update, and on mount restore it. That way when they re-enter, we first restore from IndexedDB (so we have something to show), then the WebSocket connects and we get live updates."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8015
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8016,
            "text": "Actually re-reading the use-connection-data again - we have an effect that restores closedConnections from IndexedDB when subscriptionCacheKey is set. The condition is \"if ((current.closedConnections?.length ?? 0) \u003e 0) return current\" - so we only inject closed from storage when current has no closed. So when we mount fresh, current is initConnData (empty closedConnections), so we do inject closed from IndexedDB. So closed connections should appear. But activeConnections are never persisted - they're only in memory. So when we unmount and remount, activeConnections is empty. So the \"nothing\" the user sees might be when they're on the \"active\" tab - we have no active connections to show until the next WebSocket message. So the fix is to also persist activeConnections (the last snapshot) and restore it on mount, so when re-entering we show the last known active list until the WebSocket updates."

            "text": "   Unless: when the user switches mode, does the app **leave** ConnectionsActivity? If the user is on the proxy page and switches mode there, they're not on ConnectionsActivity. So ConnectionsActivity might be in the back stack with its design still in memory. But the ticker only runs while the select is running - and that's in the main() of ConnectionsActivity. So when the user navigates away from ConnectionsActivity to switch mode, does the activity get destroyed? If the activity is destroyed, the coroutine main() is cancelled. When the user comes back to ConnectionsActivity, a new instance is created, new ConnectionsDesign, lastSnapshot = null. Then refreshConnections is called, we get the current snapshot (already after mode switch, fewer connections). We have previous = null, so we don't add anything to closed. That's the bug!"

            "text": "   So the fix is: persist the last snapshot (or at least the list of connection IDs) when we update it, and when patchConnections is called with previous == null, load the last snapshot from storage and use it as \"previous\" for the purpose of computing newly closed.
